analysis.classification_reasoning: Stock ran from $4.70 to an $8.60 intraday high (+83%) and closed the week at $7.21, retaining only 64% of peak gains with Thursday giving back 4.3% on declining volume. There's a partial fundamental anchor — a mid-impact analyst note flagging Wall Street warming to Rackspace's AI pivot — but two of three scan syntheses explicitly flagged the move as unexplained by news, pointing to options/short-cover dynamics on a sub-$10 name. Volume did persist on the up days (1.16M and 931K shares Tue/Wed vs ~500K baseline), which keeps this out of pure fakeout territory.
analysis.prediction_rationale: Base case is consolidation between $6.20 (Tuesday's breakout close) and $8.00 over the next 2-3 weeks as the AI-pivot narrative is now partially priced and there's no scheduled catalyst to force a directional resolution before August earnings. The -4.3% Thursday on rising rel-volume (1.06x) signals distribution at the highs, but the prior breakout shelf at ~$5.90 from June 15 should act as a floor on any flush. Without a fresh news catalyst, momentum funds typically let names like this digest a +53% week rather than chase.

analysis.prediction_rationale: Base case: INTC consolidates $120-135 over the next 2-3 weeks as the Apple/foundry narrative needs hard contractual detail to push higher, and a +35% week on light volume invites profit-taking. The thesis shift is real (foundry turnaround now has tangible execution markers), but the next leg requires Q2 earnings (late July) to validate. Tuesday's -8.45% intra-week air pocket on near-average volume is the warning that the tape is thin both ways.

analysis.classification_reasoning: This is real (downside) momentum, not a fakeout: FOXA gapped from $65.85 to a $54.76 close on 6/15 on 1.53M shares (~9x normal) following the $22B Roku acquisition announcement, then continued lower three more sessions to $51.30 before a weak $0.86 bounce. Of the 22% weekly decline, ~94% was retained into Friday's close (peak-to-close retention -6.6% means the drop stuck). Volume persisted at 4-9x normal across four straight days — that's distribution, not a one-day panic.
analysis.prediction_rationale: The deal-driven repricing is largely done in one violent leg; RSI ~27 and Thursday's higher low ($50.69) into a green Friday suggest sellers are exhausted near $51. But there's no catalyst to reclaim the $65 pre-deal level — every rally will be sold by holders who view the Roku price tag as value-destructive (Eisman publicly skeptical). Base case is a $50-56 grind for 2-4 weeks until earnings or regulatory clarity forces a re-rate.

analysis.classification_reasoning: Monday's gap to 136.25 (the week high) was fully unwound and then some — close at 112.57 means retention of peak was negative (-51% per heuristic) and the stock finished the week down 6.64% below the prior Friday's 123.95. Distribution pattern (higher volume on red days, failed breakout above 130s), close near week low at 112.57, and the 107.85 Thursday low is the next obvious test.
